summary: The provided information does not establish HbA1c as an ideal biomarker for detecting well-controlled diabetes; while it reflects long-term glucose trends, there are no supporting claims confirming its completeness or accuracy in capturing optimal metabolic status. Complications linked with uncontrolled diabetes highlight gaps where other markers may offer better insight into true glycaemic management outcomes.
